user_prompt: Our privacy policy is 2 years old. Generate a checklist of what to update for current GDPR + ePrivacy.
actual_output:
# GDPR + ePrivacy Privacy Policy Update Checklist

**Last Updated:** 2024 | **Applies to:** EU/EEA data subjects

---

## 1. LAWFUL BASIS DISCLOSURES — Art. 13(1)(c) & 6

### Current State Assessment
- [ ] Policy identifies **specific lawful basis** for each processing activity (not just "we process data")
- [ ] Distinguishes between: contract performance, legal obligation, legitimate interest, consent, vital interest, public task
- [ ] If using **legitimate interest**, includes brief explanation of why it's necessary and balances against data subject rights

### Required Updates
- [ ] Add lawful basis table mapping each purpose to its legal ground:
  | Purpose | Data | Lawful Basis | Retention |
  |---------|------|--------------|-----------|
  | Account creation | Email, name | Contract performance | Duration of account |
  | Marketing emails | Email | Consent (or legitimate interest if existing customer) | Until withdrawal |
  | Analytics | Usage data, IP | Consent (if tracking cookies) or legitimate interest (aggregated only) | [Specify] |

- [ ] Clarify: **Consent is NOT a catch-all basis** — only use if data subject has genuine choice to refuse without service degradation
- [ ] If relying on legitimate interest, document the balancing test (even briefly in policy)

---

## 2. TRANSPARENCY & FAIRNESS — Art. 13 & 14

### Current State Assessment
- [ ] Policy is written in **plain, understandable language** (not legal jargon)
- [ ] Information is organized by **purpose**, not by data type
- [ ] Policy is **concise** (not overwhelming wall of text)

### Required Updates
- [ ] Add **layered privacy notice** structure:
  - **Layer 1 (Essential):** Who we are, what we collect, why, how long we keep it, your rights
  - **Layer 2 (Detailed):** Lawful basis, legitimate interest balancing, automated decision-making, profiling
  - **Layer 3 (Reference):** Full processor list, sub-processor policy, DPA availability

- [ ] Use **clear headings** matching GDPR Article 13 requirements (not marketing-speak)
- [ ] Add **visual summary** (infographic or table) of key processing activities
- [ ] Ensure policy is **accessible** (readable font size, mobile-friendly, available in user's language if applicable)

---

## 3. IDENTITY & CONTACT INFORMATION — Art. 13(1)(a) & 14(1)(a)

### Current State Assessment
- [ ] Policy clearly identifies the **controller** (your organization)
- [ ] Includes **contact details** for the controller
- [ ] Lists **Data Protection Officer (DPO)** contact if applicable

### Required Updates
- [ ] Add/verify:
  - [ ] Full legal name and registered address of controller
  - [ ] Email and phone for privacy inquiries (dedicated privacy contact, not just support)
  - [ ] DPO contact (if mandatory under Art. 37) or privacy point-of-contact
  - [ ] Supervisory authority contact (link to relevant EU data protection authority)

- [ ] If you have a **representative in the EU** (Art. 27), include their contact
- [ ] If you're a **processor**, clarify relationship to controller and provide controller's contact

---

## 4. DATA SUBJECT RIGHTS — Art. 13(2)(b) & 15-22

### Current State Assessment
- [ ] Policy explains the **right to access** (Art. 15)
- [ ] Policy explains the **right to rectification** (Art. 16)
- [ ] Policy explains the **right to erasure** ("right to be forgotten") (Art. 17)
- [ ] Policy explains the **right to restrict processing** (Art. 18)
- [ ] Policy explains the **right to data portability** (Art. 20)
- [ ] Policy explains the **right to object** (Art. 21)
- [ ] Policy explains rights related to **automated decision-making & profiling** (Art. 22)

### Required Updates
- [ ] Add **dedicated section** titled "Your Rights" with:
  - [ ] **Right to access:** "You can request a copy of your personal data we hold"
  - [ ] **Right to rectification:** "You can correct inaccurate data"
  - [ ] **Right to erasure:** "You can request deletion (with exceptions for legal obligations, legitimate interests)"
  - [ ] **Right to restrict:** "You can limit how we use your data while we verify disputes"
  - [ ] **Right to portability:** "You can request your data in a portable format to move to another service"
  - [ ] **Right to object:** "You can object to processing for marketing or legitimate interest purposes"
  - [ ] **Right to withdraw consent:** "If we rely on consent, you can withdraw it anytime"
  - [ ] **Right to lodge complaint:** "You can file a complaint with your supervisory authority [link]"

- [ ] Include **how to exercise rights:**
  - [ ] Email address or form for Data Subject Access Requests (DSARs)
  - [ ] Expected response timeframe (30 days under Art. 12)
  - [ ] What information you'll need to verify identity

- [ ] Clarify **limitations** (e.g., "erasure may not apply if we have a legal obligation to retain data")

---

## 5. COOKIES & TRACKING — ePrivacy Directive + GDPR Art. 7

### Current State Assessment
- [ ] Policy discloses **all cookies and tracking technologies** used
- [ ] Distinguishes between **essential** (no consent needed) and **non-essential** (consent required)
- [ ] Explains **purpose** of each cookie/tracker

### Required Updates
- [ ] Add **cookie table** with:
  | Cookie Name | Type | Purpose | Retention | Consent Required |
  |-------------|------|---------|-----------|------------------|
  | session_id | Essential | Session management | Session | No |
  | _ga | Analytics | Google Analytics tracking | 2 years | Yes |
  | marketing_pixel | Marketing | Retargeting ads | 1 year | Yes |

- [ ] Clarify:
  - [ ] **Essential cookies** (authentication, security, load balancing) do NOT require consent
  - [ ] **Analytics cookies** require consent (unless anonymized/aggregated only)
  - [ ] **Marketing/retargeting cookies** require explicit consent
  - [ ] **Third-party cookies** (from ad networks, social media) require consent

- [ ] Add **cookie consent mechanism:**
  - [ ] Consent banner appears **before** non-essential cookies are set
  - [ ] Users can **granularly accept/reject** cookie categories (not just "accept all")
  - [ ] "Reject all" button is **equally prominent** as "Accept all"
  - [ ] Consent is **freely given** (no dark patterns, no pre-ticked boxes)

- [ ] Link to **detailed cookie policy** or provide in appendix

---

## 6. DATA RETENTION — Art. 5(1)(e)

### Current State Assessment
- [ ] Policy specifies **retention periods** for each data category
- [ ] Retention periods are **justified** (not indefinite)

### Required Updates
- [ ] Add **retention schedule** table:
  | Data Category | Purpose | Retention Period | Justification |
  |---------------|---------|------------------|---------------|
  | Account data | Service provision | Duration of account + 1 year | Legal obligation (tax) |
  | Transaction logs | Fraud prevention | 3 years | Legal obligation (financial) |
  | Marketing emails | Consent-based marketing | Until withdrawal or 2 years inactivity | Legitimate interest |
  | Analytics | Website optimization | 26 months | Legitimate interest |

- [ ] Clarify:
  - [ ] How data is **deleted or anonymized** after retention period
  - [ ] **Exceptions** (e.g., data retained for legal claims, regulatory compliance)
  - [ ] How users can request **early deletion** (subject to legal holds)

---

## 7. INTERNATIONAL DATA TRANSFERS — Art. 44-50

### Current State Assessment
- [ ] Policy discloses if data is transferred **outside the EU/EEA**
- [ ] Policy explains the **safeguard mechanism** (adequacy decision, SCCs, BCRs, etc.)

### Required Updates
- [ ] Add **transfer disclosure:**
  - [ ] List countries where data is transferred (e.g., "USA, Canada, Australia")
  - [ ] Explain **safeguard mechanism:**
    - [ ] **Adequacy decision** (
judge_reasoning: The chatbot provided a comprehensive and detailed checklist for updating a privacy policy, directly addressing the user's request while maintaining its persona.
